explanation: This command uses a standard invocation of cmd.exe with the /c flag to execute a simple command. It does not include path traversal patterns like '/../../', so it should not trigger the Sigma rule.
This command uses the /k flag to execute a command and keep the command prompt open. It is a standard usage of cmd.exe without any path traversal or suspicious patterns.
This command substitutes the long option '/c' with the short option '-c', which is functionally equivalent. It remains benign and should not trigger the Sigma rule.
This is a basic and common usage of cmd.exe with the /c flag. It is straightforward and should not be flagged by the Sigma rule.
This command includes a path traversal pattern using '..\..\', which is the Windows equivalent of '/../../'. It is designed to trigger the Sigma rule for testing purposes.
